description_long: This object consists of four irregularly shaped, dark brown stones with a matte, slightly rough surface. The stones vary in size, with the largest being rounded and bulky, while the smallest is more compact and angular. Subtle surface texturing and natural indentations are visible, suggesting weathering and exposure to outdoor elements. The coloration is mostly uniform, with some lighter brown highlights and faint mottling, giving a realistic stone appearance. The stones are grouped loosely, as if scattered or placed intentionally as a barrier or obstacle. Their form and finish suggest they could be used in urban parks, along pathways, or as decorative yet functional barriers.
description: A group of four dark brown, irregularly shaped stones with a matte, slightly rough surface and natural weathering.
description_view_0: Three stones are prominently visible, with the largest stone showing a rounded, bulky form and the others appearing smaller and more angular.
description_view_1: All four stones are clearly visible, displaying their varied sizes and shapes, with the largest stone appearing more elongated.
description_view_2: The stones are seen from above, highlighting their irregular outlines and the spatial arrangement between them.
description_view_3: Two of the stones dominate the view, showing their broad, flat surfaces and subtle surface texturing.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or grouped as a low barrier
attribute_pattern: Subtle mottling and faint natural veining, with irregular, organic surface texture.
attribute_weathering: Slightly rough and matte, with minor surface indentations indicating moderate weathering.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with landscaped areas, often placed alongside grass, gravel, or pavement for visual and functional integration.
